view.s332.h1.title: // § 332 SUB LIQUIDATION (TAX-FREE)
view.s332.hint.intro: § 332: Parent corp recognizes NO gain / loss on liquidation of ≥ 80%-owned subsidiary. § 337: Sub recognizes NO gain / loss on distribution to ≥ 80% parent. § 334(b): Parent inherits SUB's INSIDE BASIS in assets — NO step-up. § 381: Parent inherits NOL, E&P, credits, methods. Requires PLAN of liquidation + complete within ONE TAX YEAR (or 3 years per IRS approval). Minority shareholders: § 331 normal taxable. Insolvent sub: § 332 does NOT apply (parent recognizes loss under § 165).
view.s332.insol.no_332: § 332 NOT available if sub insolvent (no property distributed for stock)
view.s332.insol.no_337: Sub still recognizes gain / loss on asset distribution (§ 337 not avail)
view.s332.insol.no_381: NO § 381 carryover — sub attributes lost (NOL, E&P, credits)
view.s332.insol.s165: Parent recognizes worthless stock loss under § 165(g)(1) — ordinary if affiliated
view.s332.insol.timing: Worthlessness identified — usually formal dissolution or bankruptcy
view.s332.insol.tradeoff: Tradeoff: parent gets ordinary loss BUT sub's attributes lost forever
view.s332.label.outside: Parent outside basis in sub stock ($)
view.s332.minority_note: Minority shareholders (< 20%) DO NOT get § 332 — they recognize gain / loss under § 331 (sale or exchange treatment). Plan for separate tax computation at minority level.
view.s332.req.cessation: Sub ceases to exist for tax purposes (often state-law dissolution)
view.s332.req.foreign_sub: Foreign sub: § 367(b) regulations apply — may trigger inclusion of all E&P
view.s332.req.full_payment: Property distributed in complete cancellation of stock
view.s332.req.minority_taxable: Minority shareholders (< 20%) get § 331 fully taxable treatment
view.s332.req.ownership: Parent corp owns ≥ 80% of sub stock by vote AND value (§ 1504(a)(2))
view.s332.req.plan: Plan of liquidation adopted (formal corporate action)
view.s332.req.timing: Complete within 1 tax year of plan, OR up to 3 years w/ approval § 332(b)(3)
view.s332.s381.exception: SRLY rules limit pre-acq NOL use against post-acq income (cons. ret. only)
view.s332.s381.nol: NOL: parent inherits, subject to § 382 limitation if ownership change in sub
view.s336.disqualified_note: Loss DISALLOWED — § 336(d)(1) disqualified property + related-party distribution. Allocate loss-property to UNRELATED shareholders, OR sell BEFORE liquidation, OR avoid § 351 contributions within 2 yrs of planned liquidation.
view.s336.h1.title: // § 336 CORP LIQUIDATION
view.s336.hint.intro: § 336(a): Corp recognizes GAIN AND LOSS on liquidating distribution at FMV. Contrast § 311 (non-liquidating, gain ONLY). § 336(d)(1): NO LOSS on distribution of 'disqualified property' (contributed in § 351 / capital contrib within 2 yrs to related party). § 336(d)(2): 'Tax avoidance' loss reduced if basis > FMV. Shareholder side: § 331 — gain / loss on stock; FMV basis in distributed property. § 332 alternate: tax-free if 80%+ parent (§ 337 for sub).
view.s336.loss.allocation: Loss allocated pro-rata across distributee shareholders
view.s336.loss.disqual_period: 5-year period: contribution + tax avoidance presumed within 2 yrs
view.s336.loss.disqualified: 'Disqualified property' = property contributed in § 351 / capital contrib + tax-avoidance purpose
view.s336.loss.related: Related party: § 267 — > 50% owner, family, controlled corps
view.s336.loss.s336d1: § 336(d)(1): NO LOSS on distribution of disqualified property to related party (§ 267)
view.s336.loss.s336d2: § 336(d)(2): 'tax avoidance' loss reduced if asset's basis > FMV at contribution date
view.s336.loss.s336d3: § 336(d)(3): no loss to 80% parent under § 332 (general non-recognition)
view.s336.s336e.basis_step: Buyer gets stepped-up basis to AGUB (similar to 338(h)(10))
view.s336.s336e.consent: Seller-side election; no buyer consent required
view.s336.s336e.election: Election: attached to seller's tax return for year of sale
view.s336.s336e.gain_seller: Gain recognized at seller / consolidated group level
view.s336.s336e.s_corp: S-corp target OR consolidated sub eligible (similar to § 338(h)(10))
view.s336.s336e.s_corp_basis: S-corp shareholders adjust basis for inside-built gain
view.s336.s336e.scope: Stock sale to UNRELATED party by single SELLER (not buyer) recharacterized as asset sale
view.s336.s336e.use_case: Useful when buyer is reluctant to join § 338(h)(10) but seller wants asset-sale treatment
view.s336.share.amount: Amount realized = FMV of property + cash + liability discharged (not assumed)
view.s336.share.depreciation_recap: No depreciation recapture passes through to shareholder (corp recognizes at § 336)
view.s336.share.gain_loss: Gain / loss = amount realized − stock basis (capital character)
view.s336.share.holding: Holding period: from original acquisition; LTCG if > 1 yr
view.s336.share.installment: § 453(h): installment note received in liquidation reported on installment method
view.s336.share.minority: Minority shareholders (< 20%): always § 331 (even if 80%+ parent uses § 332)
view.s336.share.s453B_d: § 453B(d): NO recognition on dist of installment notes (deferred)
view.s338.cls.actively_traded: Class II: actively traded securities + CDs
view.s338.cls.cash: Class I: cash + bank deposits (no allocation)
view.s338.cls.goodwill: Class VII: goodwill + going concern value
view.s338.cls.intangible_non_goodwill: Class VI: intangibles not in Class VII (§ 197, customer lists)
view.s338.cls.receivables: Class III: receivables, mortgages, credit card
view.s338.cls.residual: Allocation: by FMV within each class, residual to Class VI/VII (goodwill)
view.s338.election_note: Election produces positive NPV. Coordinate § 1060 / § 338 residual allocation method to maximize Class VI/VII (15-yr § 197 amortization) over Class IV/V (longer depreciation).
view.s338.h1.title: // § 338 STOCK→ASSET ELECTION
view.s338.hint.intro: Stock purchase treated as ASSET PURCHASE for tax. § 338(g): unilateral by buyer (Buyer pays target's immediate gain tax with no offset to Seller). § 338(h)(10): joint election (Buyer + Seller) for S-corp OR consolidated-group target — gain recognized at SELLER level (one tax). Target gets step-up to AGUB (Aggregated Grossed-Up Basis). Tradeoff: immediate gain tax at acquisition vs step-up (depreciation / amortization) recovery. Filed on Form 8023.
view.s338.loss.consolidated: LOSE: Acquirer can't use sub's losses against group (SRLY) anyway
view.s338.loss.high_appreciation: LOSE: Target has high appreciated assets (huge gain tax)
view.s338.loss.nol_destruction: LOSE: Target has NOLs that would otherwise carry over (lost in election)
view.s338.loss.qsst_disabled: LOSE: S-corp ESBT / QSST trust beneficiary may disqualify
view.s338.no_election_note: Election cost exceeds step-up benefit. Likely better off WITHOUT § 338 election — accept stock-purchase treatment, retain target's NOLs / E&P, no immediate gain tax.
view.s338.win.foreign_338g: § 338(g) wins for foreign target (gain often deferred / minimal foreign-tax burden)
view.s338.win.high_basis: WIN: Target has high inside basis already (small step-up cost)
view.s338.win.high_intangibles: WIN: Target has significant intangibles → § 197 15-year amortization
view.s338.win.s_corp: § 338(h)(10) win for S-corp targets — single level gain
view.s351.boot.allocation: Multiple properties: allocate boot proportionally by FMV
view.s351.boot.basis: Podstawa przekazująca w podmiocie = podstawa majątkowa + zysk - boot - pasywa
view.s351.boot.cash: Otrzymana gotówka + nieruchomość inna niż magazynowa = BOOT, zysk rozpoznany w zakresie rozruchu
view.s351.boot.character: Boot gain character = character of property (cap vs ordinary)
view.s351.boot.gain_limit: Recognize LESSER of (gain realized) OR (boot received)
view.s351.boot.liabilities: Przejęte zobowiązania = BOOT dla § 357 (c) zysk, jeśli zobowiązania > podstawa (tylko spółka)
view.s351.boot.no_loss: NIGDY nie rozpoznaj straty na § 351 / § 721 (tylko zysk)
view.s351.boot.partnership_liability: Zobowiązania spółki: zwiększenie/zmniejszenie podstawy wspólnika zgodnie z § 752
view.s351.boot.property: Other property = boot — basis carries over
view.s351.boot.securities: Securities (notes / bonds) treated as boot under § 354(a)(2)
view.s351.boot.short_term: Short-term debt instruments treated as cash
view.s351.card.new_basis: Nowa podstawa udziału w kapitale zakładowym
view.s351.h1.title: // § 351 / § 721 FORMACJA WOLNA PODATKOWO
view.s351.hint.intro: Wnieś majątek do spółki / spółki osobowej w zamian za udziały własnościowe — generalnie nie uznaje się żadnego zysku. § 351: wpłacający + współwłaściciele łącznie posiadają ≥ 80% po przeniesieniu. § 721: wpłata do spółki zwolniona z podatku (bez badania kontrolnego). § 357 boot trap: zobowiązania przejęte przez podmiot przekraczające podstawę wpłacającego uruchamiają GAIN. § 1032: jednostka nie rozpoznaje zysku z tytułu emisji własnych akcji na majątek.
view.s351.liab.s357b: § 357(b) tax avoidance / non-business purpose: liab = boot
view.s351.liab.s357c: § 357(c) liabilities exceed basis: gain to extent of excess
view.s351.liab.s357c_3: § 357(c)(3) deductible liabilities (A/P, accrued exp) NOT counted in liab
view.s351.liab.s357d: § 357(d) — recourse vs nonrecourse — only deemed assumed if economically borne
view.s351.liab.s358d: § 358(d) treat assumed liability as money received for basis (for stock)
view.s351.liab.s362e_1: § 362(e)(1) limited to FMV for net built-in loss property
view.s351.note.disregarded: Single-member LLC = podmiot pomijany – brak zdarzenia podlegającego opodatkowaniu, właściciel bezpośrednio posiada nieruchomość do celów podatkowych.
view.s351.rel.s367a: § 367(a) outbound transfers to foreign corp — gain recognition
view.s351.rel.s7701l: § 7701(l) anti-conduit for cross-border § 351 with foreign acquirer
view.s351.rel.s897: § 897 FIRPTA — US real property to foreign corp
view.s351.req.control: Transferor(s) immediately after exchange CONTROL 80%+ (voting + total)
view.s351.req.related_steps: Related step transactions: integrated under § 351 (Rev. Rul. 70-140)
view.s351.req.s368b: § 368(b) — single class of stock not required (vs S-corp)
view.s351.req.s368c: § 368(c) control = 80% of voting + 80% of each non-voting class
view.s351.req.stock: Receives ONLY STOCK in exchange (boot allowed but recognized)
view.s351.req.transferors_aggregate: "Transferors" aggregate — all who transfer in single transaction
view.s351.tbl.t_formula: Property basis + gain recognized − boot − liabilities
view.s355.case.ebay_paypal: eBay → PayPal (2015): clean Type D divisive
view.s355.case.morris_trust_origin: Morris Trust v. Commissioner (1966): merger-spin combo upheld → § 355(e) enacted 1997
view.s355.case.no_ruling: 'No-rule' areas: 2003 Rev. Proc. 2003-48 reduced rulings significantly
view.s355.case.private_letter: PLR practice: 6-9 months pre-spin work; ruling cost $40-50K
view.s355.h1.title: // § 355 SPIN-OFF / SPLIT-OFF
view.s355.hint.intro: Tax-free distribution of CONTROLLED CORP STOCK. Requirements: (1) 5-yr active business EACH side, (2) control (80%) distributed, (3) not a device for dividend, (4) business purpose, (5) continuity of interest (50%+ owners). Spin-off: pro-rata (shareholders keep both). Split-off: surrender Distributing stock. Split-up: Distributing liquidates → shareholders get Sub1 + Sub2. Anti-abuse: § 355(d) 50% acquisition triggers gain. § 355(e) Morris Trust.
view.s355.morris_note: § 355(e) Morris Trust triggered: 50%+ acquisition of either D or C within 2-yr window (before / after) causes Distributing to recognize gain (not loss). Plan against post-spin acquisitions for at least 24 months to preserve tax-free status.
view.s355.req.active: Active Trade or Business 5-year rule: § 355(b) — BOTH Distributing + Controlled engaged for 5 years
view.s355.req.cobe: Continuity of Business Enterprise: BOTH sides continue historic business
view.s355.req.continuity: Continuity of Interest: 50%+ Distributing shareholders retain stock in spun-off
view.s355.req.control: Distribute Control: 80% vote AND 80% value of Controlled stock
view.s355.req.device: Not Used Principally as Device for distribution of E&P (§ 355(a)(1)(B))
view.s355.req.no_355e: No § 355(e) 'Morris Trust' 50% acquisition of either D or C within 2-yr window
view.s357.a.basis_reduce: Transferor's stock basis REDUCED by liabilities assumed
view.s357.a.bookkeeping: No current tax, but built-in gain preserved
view.s357.a.example: Example: transfer property basis $100K, FMV $300K, liab $80K → no gain; stock basis = $20K
view.s357.a.no_boot: Assumption of liabilities NOT treated as boot
view.s357.a.rationale: Economic substance: transferor's net economic position unchanged
view.s357.b.all_boot: ENTIRE liability amount treated as boot → gain to extent of FMV − basis ratio
view.s357.b.cash_out: Transferor extracts cash via debt → § 357(b) more likely applies
view.s357.b.intent: IRS often finds avoidance when liability incurred shortly before transfer
view.s357.b.recent_debt: Recent debt + cash extraction = 'tax avoidance' presumption
view.s357.b.refinance: Refinancing before incorporation: scrutinized as bootstrap
view.s357.b.trigger: Tax avoidance motive OR no business purpose for transfer of liability
view.s357.c.basic: Liabilities ASSUMED > TOTAL BASIS of property transferred → recognize gain
view.s357.c.basis_floor: Transferor's stock basis cannot go below zero — § 357(c) prevents negative basis
view.s357.c.character: Character: same as if property sold (capital, ordinary, recapture)
view.s357.c.s357c_3: § 357(c)(3): liabilities arising from deductible-when-paid items NOT counted (accounts payable)
view.s357.c.s361_reorg: § 361 reorg context: § 357(c) does NOT apply to plain D reorgs
view.s357.c.solution_boot: Workaround: take back NOTE / boot for excess (recognize gain anyway but more flexibility)
view.s357.h1.title: // § 357 LIABILITIES IN § 351
view.s357.hint.intro: § 357(a): Liability assumption generally NOT boot (no immediate gain). § 357(b): Tax avoidance / no business purpose → ENTIRE liability treated as boot → gain to transferor. § 357(c): Liabilities EXCEED basis → gain to extent of excess. Basis impact: transferor's stock basis = property basis − liabilities; transferee corporation inherits transferor's basis in property. Coordinate with § 351 control test.
view.s357.label.other_basis: Transferor's basis in OTHER prop transferred ($)
view.s357.s357c_note: § 357(c) gain triggered — liabilities exceeded basis. Workarounds: (1) contribute additional high-basis property to absorb excess, (2) transferor retains liability instead of letting corp assume it, (3) take back installment note to spread gain.
view.s362.e2.applies: Applies when AGGREGATE adjusted basis of contributed property > AGGREGATE FMV
view.s362.e2.benefit: Election benefit: preserves loss inside corp where it may be realized later
view.s362.e2.binding: Election BINDING — cannot be revoked except with IRS consent
view.s362.e2.default: DEFAULT (no election): reduce CORP's basis to AGGREGATE FMV — pro-rata across NBIL properties only
view.s362.e2.election: § 362(e)(2)(C) ELECTION: reduce TRANSFEROR's OUTSIDE stock basis instead → preserves corp basis at carryover
view.s362.e2.purpose: Anti-abuse: prevents shifting same loss to both transferor stock + corp asset basis
view.s362.e2.s382: Election may HURT later if ownership change triggers § 382 limitation
view.s362.e2.timing: Election made by ATTACHING STATEMENT to timely filed return for year of transfer
view.s362.h1.title: // § 362 CORPORATE BASIS ON TAX-FREE TRANSFER
view.s362.hint.intro: § 362(a) carryover + § 362(b) reorg + § 362(e)(2) NBIL anti-loss reduction OR election to reduce outside basis. § 1223(2) holding period tacking.

view.s362.s338.purpose: Stock acquisition treated as DEEMED ASSET ACQUISITION

view.s367a.gra.purpose: 5-year deferral if transferor enters GRA + commits to recognize gain on triggering events
view.s367a.gra.s_corp: S-corp transferor: shareholders bear gain consequences
view.s367a.gra.triggering: Triggering events: sale of stock, dispositions of property, change in basis
view.s367a.h1.title: // § 367(a) OUTBOUND TANGIBLE
view.s367a.hint.intro: US transferor recognizes GAIN on outbound transfer of property to foreign corp. § 367(a)(1) general rule: foreign transferee treated as NOT a corp → no § 351 deferral. § 367(a)(3) ACTIVE business exception (HISTORICAL, TCJA 2017 NARROWED scope — for ACTIVE TRADE / BUSINESS US TANGIBLE property). Inventory + depreciable + installment obligations + receivables: NEVER qualify for exception. Compare § 367(d) IP outbound (annual deemed royalty). § 6038B reporting required.
view.s367a.no_filing_note: § 6038B Form 926 REQUIRED for outbound transfers. Failure to file: $100,000 minimum penalty (or 10% of FMV if higher, capped at $100K). Statute of limitations remains open for 3 yrs after late filing. Penalty waived only for reasonable cause + GOOD FAITH effort.
view.s367d.comp.amortization: Annual inclusion declines as IP's productive capacity decreases
view.s367d.comp.character: Character: ORDINARY royalty income (not capital gain)
view.s367d.comp.no_capital_gain: No capital gain even on appreciated IP at transfer (annual royalty instead)
view.s367d.comp.no_loss: No loss on transfer even if FMV < basis
view.s367d.comp.royalty: Annual royalty: arm's-length comparable (CUT, Profit Split, Income Method)
view.s367d.comp.subF_overlap: Subject to subF / GILTI if held in CFC: avoid double-tax via § 78 gross-up
view.s367d.comp.transferee_basis: Transferee basis = FMV at transfer (capital expenditure)
view.s367d.comp.useful_life: Useful life: estimated economic life of IP (typically 5-15 years)
view.s367d.coord.s250: § 250 FDII: incentive to keep IP in US (13.125% effective rate)
view.s367d.coord.s367a: § 367(a)(1): tangible property outbound — gain recognition (vs § 367(d) ongoing royalty)
view.s367d.coord.s367a3: § 367(a)(3): active trade/business exception (TCJA removed for IP)
view.s367d.coord.s367b: § 367(b) inbound regulations — exit-charge for CFC liquidations
view.s367d.coord.s482: § 482 transfer pricing: same arm's-length standard applies on royalty determination
view.s367d.coord.s936: § 936 possessions tax credit — repealed but historical context
view.s367d.coord.s951a: § 951A GILTI: IP income now subject to GILTI inclusion if held in CFC
view.s367d.coord.s956: § 956 investment in US property: keeps IP from US shareholder access
view.s367d.csa.amazon_altera: Tax Court precedent: Amazon (CSA cost methodology), Altera (SBC must be shared)
view.s367d.csa.entry_fee: Foreign sub pays BUY-IN to US sub for existing IP value
view.s367d.csa.financial_substance: Each party must contribute substantial cost + receive substantial benefit
view.s367d.csa.ongoing: Ongoing R&D costs SHARED in proportion to expected benefits
view.s367d.csa.platform_contribution: Platform Contribution: existing IP buy-in determined via Income Method / CUT / DCM
view.s367d.csa.purpose: Avoid § 367(d) by SHARING development costs of new IP
view.s367d.csa.stock_based: Stock-based compensation MUST be included in cost base (Altera v. Comm'r)
view.s367d.csa_note: CSA in place: § 367(d) deemed royalty AVOIDED. Buy-in payment + ongoing cost share. Critical: stock-based compensation MUST be included in cost base (Altera). Document IP development costs + benefit allocation contemporaneously. Tax Court has reviewed Amazon + Altera challenges to enforce arm's-length CSA pricing.
view.s367d.def.goodwill: Goodwill and going concern value — TCJA NEW
view.s367d.def.licenses: Licenses, permits, other rights granted by governmental units
view.s367d.def.other: Methods of doing business, business records, operating systems
view.s367d.def.patents: Patents, inventions, formulas, processes, designs, patterns, knowhow
view.s367d.def.trademarks: Trademarks, trade names, brand names, franchises
view.s367d.h1.title: // § 367(d) IP OUTBOUND
view.s367d.hint.intro: US transferor of INTANGIBLE PROPERTY to foreign corp must include ANNUAL DEEMED ROYALTY over IP's useful life. TCJA 2017 expansion: 'intangible' now includes workforce in place, goodwill, going concern, customer lists. Royalty = arm's-length annual amount (parallel to § 482 transfer pricing). Cost Sharing Arrangement (CSA) § 482 alternative — buy-in + ongoing R&D shares. 2017 effective: applies post-Dec 31 2017 transfers. § 6038B reporting. Avoid: keep IP in US, or CSA, or royalty structure (not outbound transfer).
view.s368.h1.title: // § 368 REORGANIZATIONS
view.s368.hint.intro: Seven types of tax-free reorganizations § 368(a)(1)(A)-(G). Common requirements: Continuity of Interest (40%+ stock consideration), Continuity of Business Enterprise, Business Purpose, Plan of Reorganization. Carry over: § 381 sub attributes (NOL, E&P, credits). § 382 limitation on NOL post-ownership-change. Boot = non-stock consideration recognized as gain (not loss). Form 8806 filed for B / C / D acquisitive reorgs.
view.s368.jd.business_purpose: Business Purpose: corp-level (not solely tax-motivated). Gregory v. Helvering (1935)
view.s368.jd.cobe: Continuity of Business Enterprise (COBE): continue HISTORIC business OR use HISTORIC assets in business
view.s368.jd.coi: Continuity of Interest (COI): 40%+ stock consideration; measured at time of binding agreement (post-2005)
view.s368.jd.economic_substance: § 7701(o) codified economic substance doctrine — meaningful change required + non-tax purpose
view.s368.jd.plan: Plan of Reorganization: formal corporate action documenting steps
view.s368.jd.step_transaction: Step transaction: integrated steps treated as one (Smith v. Comm'r, Walden v. Comm'r)
view.s368.jd.substance_form: Substance over form: tax follows economic substance
view.s368.no_qualify_note: Reorg requirements NOT met → TAXABLE transaction. Full target gain recognized at corporate level (20%+ LTCG) + shareholder level (sale treatment) → double tax.
view.s368.s382.bankruptcy_exception: § 382(l)(5): G reorg in bankruptcy — historic creditors as continuing shareholders
view.s368.s382.built_in_gain: RBIG (recognized built-in gain) adjustments increase limit during 5-yr period
view.s368.s382.limit: Annual NOL limit: target value × federal long-term tax-exempt rate (~4-5%)
view.s368.s382.notice2003_65: Notice 2003-65 + 2019 proposed regs detail built-in gain measurement
view.s368.s382.trigger: Ownership change: > 50 pct-point shift over 3-yr test period among 5% shareholders
view.s36b.h1.title: // § 36B ACA Ulga podatkowa SKŁADKI
view.s36b.hint.intro: Kredyt zwrotny umożliwiający zmniejszenie miesięcznych składek na ubezpieczenie zdrowotne na Healthcare.gov. Pre-IRA: dostępne tylko 100-400% FPL (klif). ARPA/IRA 2022-2025: rozszerzony na WSZYSTKIE dochody przy składce > 8,5% MAGI. Uzgodniono na formularzu 8962. Krytyczne dla osób prowadzących działalność na własny rachunek – utrata rocznego APTC z powodu wysokiego zysku kosztuje tysiące. Zachód słońca w 2026 r.: 400% zwrotów z klifów FPL.
view.s36b.plan.advance: Jeśli przekroczysz wstępne szacunki, DZIAŁAJ TERAZ, aby porzucić MAGI
view.s36b.plan.cap_repayment: Pre-ARPA: limit spłaty 200% FPL – ograniczenie ryzyka spłaty
view.s36b.plan.cliff_2026: Zwroty z klifu w 2026 r.: zejdź poniżej 400% FPL lub strać całą dotację
view.s36b.plan.harvesting: Zbiór strat w celu zrównoważenia zysków zrealizowanych przed końcem roku
view.s36b.plan.hsa: Składki na HSA (po opodatkowaniu) również zmniejszają MAGI
view.s36b.plan.roth_timing: Konwersje Time Roth dla lat o niskich dochodach
view.s36b.plan.solo_401k: Maksymalny wkład solo 401(k) w zrzucenie MAGI pod klif
view.s36b.warning.repay: Zaliczka PTC przekracza zarobione PTC → jesteś należny różnicę z powrotem na formularzu 8962. Obowiązują górne limity spłaty < 400% FPL. ≥ 400% FPL (przed IRA) = pełna spłata.
view.s38.carry_note: Unused credit carries back 1 yr (file Form 1045 / 1139 quick refund) OR forward 20 yrs. For IRA 2022 specified credits: consider § 6417 direct pay (tax-exempts) or § 6418 transferability (taxables) to monetize immediately at 88-95¢ on $1.
view.s38.comp.alcohol: § 40 Alcohol fuel mixtures + § 40A biodiesel + § 40B Sustainable Aviation Fuel
view.s38.comp.employer: § 45E Small Employer Pension Setup + § 45F Childcare + § 45R Health Insurance
view.s38.comp.investment: Investment credit § 46: § 48 ITC, § 48C, § 48E, § 47 rehab, § 48D semi mfg
view.s38.comp.renewable: § 45 Renewable Electricity PTC + § 45Y Clean Electricity PTC
view.s38.comp.research: § 41 Research credit (incremental QRE method or ASC)
view.s38.comp.work_opportunity: § 51 Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC): up to $9,600 per qualified hire
view.s38.h1.title: // § 38 GENERAL BUSINESS CREDIT
view.s38.hint.intro: Aggregator of business credits: § 41 R&D + § 45 PTC + § 48 ITC + § 45Q + § 30D + § 45W + § 45L + § 45X + Empowerment Zone + WOTC + Disabled Access + many more. Limit: net regular tax above TMT (Tentative Minimum Tax) + 25% of next $25K. Carryback 1-yr, Carryforward 20-yr (§ 39). Forms 3800 + 8000-series source forms. § 6417 direct pay + § 6418 transferability for IRA 2022 specified credits (different rules per credit).
view.s38.lim.25pct: + 25% of remaining tax above $25K (after TMT reached)
view.s38.lim.basic: Limit = Net Tax (regular) above Tentative Minimum Tax (TMT)
view.s38.lim.carry_back: Unused: carry back 1 yr first, then 20-year carryforward
view.s38.lim.eligible_small: Eligible small biz: R&D + energy + various credits can offset AMT entirely
view.s38.lim.pre_acquisition: § 39(d) pre-acquisition GBCs: SRLY-limited similar to NOL
view.s38.lim.s39_expiration: § 39 carryforward expires after 20 yrs → permanent loss
view.s38.lim.specified: 'Specified credits' (R&D portion, energy ITC, etc.) can offset AMT + TMT
view.s382.cobe.failure: If FAIL: pre-change NOLs ELIMINATED entirely (not just limited)
view.s382.cobe.purpose: Anti-trafficking: prevents acquiring loss corp + immediately shutting down
view.s382.cobe.test: Must EITHER continue historic business OR use significant historic assets
view.s382.h1.title: // § 382 NOL LIMITATION (OWNERSHIP CHANGE)
view.s382.hint.intro: Limits NOL utilization after ownership change to annual limit = FMV × LT tax-exempt rate. § 382(h) RBIG/RBIL 5-yr. § 382(c) COBE 2-yr or NOLs eliminated.
view.s382.lim.carryforward: Unused limit: carries forward to next year (cumulative)
view.s382.lim.formula: Annual limit = FMV of loss corp (immediately before change) × LT tax-exempt rate
view.s382.lim.rate: LT tax-exempt rate: highest of 3 prior months (published monthly by IRS)
view.s382.lim.s382_b_2: § 382(b)(2): in change year, prorate based on days in year
view.s382.lim.s382_e_anti_stuffing: § 382(e) anti-stuffing: redemptions/contributions in 2-year period before change reduce FMV
view.s382.lim.zero_fmv: If FMV at change is ~$0 (bankruptcy etc.): annual limit = $0, NOLs functionally lost
view.s382.oc.equity_split: Equity restructuring + recapitalization may trigger ownership change
view.s382.oc.equity_structure: Test based on lower of: voting power OR value
view.s382.oc.options: Stock options + convertible debt: treated as ownership if "in the money"
view.s382.oc.public_group: "Public group" = aggregation of all < 5% shareholders (treated as single 5% shareholder)
view.s382.oc.shareholder: "5% shareholder" includes both direct (named) + indirect (via aggregation)
view.s382.oc.testing: Testing period: 3 years ending on potential change date
view.s382.oc.threshold: "Ownership change" = > 50 PERCENTAGE POINT increase in stock owned by 5%+ shareholders
view.s382.rb.below_threshold: If below threshold: no RBIG/RBIL adjustments
view.s382.rb.purpose: 5-year window: identifies built-in gains/losses at time of change
view.s382.rb.rbig: RBIG: recognized built-in gain — ADDS to annual limit when recognized
view.s382.rb.rbil: RBIL: recognized built-in loss — subject to § 382 limit (does NOT escape)
view.s382.rb.s1374_approach: § 1374 approach: track items individually recognized in 5-year window
view.s382.rb.s338_approach: § 338 approach: hypothetical sale at change FMV identifies all gains/losses
view.s382.rb.s382_h_8: § 382(h)(8) — deferred CODI included if subject to attribute reduction
view.s382.rb.threshold: NUBIG/NUBIL threshold: lesser of $10M OR 15% of value of asset at change
view.s382.relief.f_reorganization: F-reorganization: NO ownership change (single corp continuation)
view.s382.relief.poison_pill: Poison pill / shareholder rights plan — defends against unintentional change
view.s382.relief.qstk: QSST + ESBT for S-corp ownership testing
view.s382.relief.s269_b: § 269(b)(2) — relief from § 269 if no tax avoidance purpose
view.s382.relief.s338_election: § 338 / § 336(e) election: step-up basis avoids inheriting limitation in some cases
view.s382.relief.s382_l_5: § 382(l)(5) bankruptcy: title 11 reorg — exempt if old shareholders retain 50%+
view.s382.relief.s382_l_6: § 382(l)(6) bankruptcy alternative: increased FMV based on post-reorg value
view.s401a9.ex.first_year: Pierwszy rok RMD można przełożyć na 1 kwietnia następnego roku (ale tworzy to podwójny rok RMD)
view.s401a9.ex.inherited: Odziedziczone IRA: odrębne zasady (zasada 10 lat + wyjątki EDB)

view.s401a9.ex.roth_ira: Roth IRA: BRAK RMD za życia właściciela (SECURE 2.0 wyeliminował także Roth 401(k) RMD)
view.s401a9.ex.still_working: Nadal pracuje + < 5% Właściciel: 401(k) RMD odroczone u obecnego pracodawcy
view.s401a9.ex.terminal: Choroba nieuleczalna: wyjątek SECURE 2.0 (przewidywana długość życia < 7 lat)
view.s401a9.h1.title: // § 401(a)(9) KALKULATOR RMD
view.s401a9.hint.intro: Ustawa SECURE 2019 + SECURE 2.0 2022 zmieniona RBD (wymagana data rozpoczęcia): Urodzeni ≤ 30.06.1949: wiek 70½. Urodzeni 1.7.1949 → 31.12.1950: 72 lata. Urodzeni 1951-1959: 73 lata. Urodzeni 1960+: 75 lat. RMD = saldo z 31 grudnia poprzedniego roku ÷ dzielnik oczekiwanej długości życia (Uniform Lifetime Table). QCD do 105 000 dolarów (2024) z IRA rekompensuje RMD bez wliczania do dochodu. Kara za ominięcie RMD: 25% (10%, jeśli zostanie poprawione w odpowiednim czasie).
view.s401a9.label.spouse_younger: Małżonek > 10 lat młodszy i jedyny beneficjent?
view.s401a9.note.joint: Małżonek o > 10 lat młodszy jedyny beneficjent: skorzystaj ze wspólnej tabeli oczekiwanej długości życia (dłuższy dzielnik → mniejszy RMD). Przelicz co roku.
view.s401a9.qcd.501c3: Bezpośredni transfer wyłącznie na rzecz publicznej organizacji charytatywnej 501(c)(3) (nie DAF, fundacja)
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi: Wlicza się do RMD, jeśli wiek RMD
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi_2024: Limit 105 000 USD na rok 2024 (indeksowany); SECURE 2.0, jednorazowa gra CRT/CGA o wartości 50 tys. dolarów
view.s401a9.qcd.medicare: Niższy AGI zmniejsza składki Medicare IRMAA (2 lata wstecz)
view.s401a9.qcd.muni: Niższy AGI zapobiega opodatkowaniu obligacji komunalnych / Ubezpieczeń Społecznych
view.s401a9.qcd.no_agi: Nie obejmuje kwoty z AGI – lepsza niż wyszczególnione odliczenie na cele charytatywne
view.s401a9.qcd.over_70: Dostępne w wieku 70½+, nawet jeśli RMD nie jest jeszcze wymagane
view.s401a9.qcd.spouse: Każdy z małżonków ma oddzielny limit w przypadku odrębnych kont IRA

view.s401h.doc.funeral: Pogrzeb: szczegółowe rachunki za pogrzeb + akt zgonu
